name: typescript
description: TypeScript stack: strictNullChecks, no any, branded types where useful, type tests for public API.

rules.guidance: tsconfig must have strictNullChecks: true. Null/undefined must be handled explicitly. Optional chaining (?.) and nullish coalescing (??) over manual null checks.
Avoid `any`. Use `unknown` for truly dynamic input and narrow with type guards. Use generics or union types for polymorphic code.
Use branded/nominal types for IDs and domain primitives to prevent mixing unrelated string/number values at compile time. Useful for UserId, OrderId, etc.
Add type-level tests for public API types using expect-type or tsd to catch breaking type changes before runtime tests catch them.
Exported functions should have explicit return type annotations. Inferred return types are acceptable for small private helpers but can mask breaking changes in public APIs.
